merlin.region: This bird breeds across the northern Holarctic, from boreal forests and open moorlands of North America to Eurasia. In winter, many populations migrate south to subtropical and northern tropical regions, though some urban birds forgo migration entirely.
merlin.behavior: A swift, agile hunter, this bird specialises in catching small birds — from sparrows up to doves and medium-sized shorebirds — in fast, low-level aerial pursuits, relying on surprise and speed rather than soaring.
merlin.funFact: The merlin has adapted so well to city life in parts of North America that urban populations have stopped migrating altogether, nesting on building ledges and hunting house sparrows year-round.
